{{Quote|Jax's treachery went far deeper than we could have imagined.|Kir Kanos|<ref name="ce 3" />}}
An '''attack''' on the [[Emperor's Royal Guard/Legends|Emperor's Royal Guard]] took place on [[Yinchorr/Legends|Yinchorr]] during the [[Imperial Civil War]]. The incident occurred in the wake of the destruction of [[Byss/Legends|Byss]], the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] throneworld, and the final death of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]. [[Carnor Jax]], a member of the [[Emperor's Royal Guard/Legends|Emperor's Royal Guard]] and an aspiring [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] himself, played a part in the Emperor's death, which was part of his bid for the title of Emperor.

Jax's splinter group proceeded to cleanse the Empire of Palpatine's loyalists, leading to an attack on the [[Imperial Royal Guard Academy]] on Yinchorr in an effort to kill the surviving members of the Royal Guard, who were knowledgeable of Jax's plot and were intent on retribution. The ensuing bloodbath led to the death of 18 guardsmen, as well as their leader, [[Ved Kennede]]. The sole guardsman who survived the incident, [[Kir Kanos]], vowed to carry on the guardsmen's pact to kill Jax and avenge Palpatine.

==Prelude==
{{Quote|On our lives, we swore death to the traitor.|Kir Kanos|<ref name="ce 3" />}}
Following the first death of Emperor Palpatine during the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], Palpatine's [[Emperor's Royal Guard/Legends|Royal Guardsmen]] were scattered until reconvening on [[Byss/Legends|Byss]] on [[11 ABY/Legends|11 ABY]], after their master returned in the form of a clone. Following the final death of Palpatine on [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]] that same year, many of his Royal Guardsmen also perished in the [[Destruction of Byss|destruction]] of Byss,<ref name="ce 3" /> brought on by a misfiring of the Emperor's [[Galaxy Gun]] [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]].<ref name="ee" /> Others died during the destruction of the Emperor's flagship, the ''[[Eclipse II]]''. The few survivors of the unit made their way to the [[Imperial Royal Guard Academy]] on [[Yinchorr/Legends|Yinchorr]].<ref name="ce 3" />

Ten days after Palpatine's death, a wounded straggler made his way to the Academy: one of the two guards Palpatine had taken to Onderon. He had been wounded and believed killed by the aspiring [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Rayf Ysanna]], but had, in fact, lived. By spying upon the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] interrogation of the Emperor's [[Emperor's physician|physician]] the lone Guardsmen learned that Palpatine's [[Cloning/Legends|clones]] had failed to deliberate poisoning—and that such poisoning had been done on the orders of [[Carnor Jax]], one of the Royal Guard who had not returned to Yinchorr with his comrades. The assembled Guardsmen made a promise to not rest until the traitor had been killed and Palpatine avenged.<ref name="ce 3" />

==The attack==
{{Quote|Moments later, that pact was sealed in blood as we were attacked by Imperial troops.|Kir Kanos|<ref name="ce 3" />}}
[[File:BattleOfYinchorr-CE1-i3-p19.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The Royal Guardsmen are surrounded by enemies]]
At that moment, scores of Imperial [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] dispatched by Jax stormed the Academy, and the Guardsmen were forced to defend themselves. Outnumbered more than ten to one, they were slowly overrun, losing even their trainer [[Ved Kennede]], until only two were left: [[Kir Kanos]] and [[Kile Hannad]]. Deciding that one of them should sacrifice his life so the other could live to fulfill the blood oath against Jax, they flipped a coin. The coin toss named Kanos as the one who would hunt down and kill Jax, leaving Kile Hannad to die a warrior's death. As Hannad charged the remaining Imperial forces, Kanos slipped quietly from the Academy before it was demolished, and escaped from Yinchorr to the portworld [[Phaeda/Legends|Phaeda]] as Hannad enjoyed a warrior's death.<ref name="ce 3" />

==Aftermath==
{{Quote|As long as he lives, our plans can never come to fruition.|Carnor Jax|<ref name="ce 1">[[Crimson Empire 1|''Crimson Empire'' 1]]</ref>}}
Jax later learned from an analysis of the dead that Kanos had escaped, and ordered his forces under [[General/Legends|General]] [[Redd Wessel]] to continue the search for the last man who knew the secret of how Jax had assumed the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] throne. Jax considered Kanos to be a thorn in his plans for Galactic domination, and possibly the only man in the galaxy capable of undoing his work.<ref name="ce 1" />

Kanos kept a low profile on [[Phaeda/Legends|Phaeda]] for a period of time following the massacre, where he inadvertently became entangled in the conflict between the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] and the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].<ref name="ce 1" /> His involvement would ultimately help him fulfill the blood pact against Jax, ultimately slaying him in [[The Squall/Legends|The Squall]] on Yinchorr.<ref name="ce 6">[[Crimson Empire 6|''Crimson Empire'' 6]]</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The massacre of the Royal Guardsmen on Yinchorr was first depicted in the [[Crimson Empire 3|third issue]] of the [[Dark Horse Comics]] mini-series ''[[Star Wars: Crimson Empire]]''. The incident serves as the catalyst for the conflict between Kir Kanos, the series's protagonistic anti-hero, and Carnor Jax, the series's antagonist.
